_comment: One-line descriptions appended to messages in the bottom help bar ('<message> - <description>'). Keys are the ModuleWindow control names (shared controls share one key; per-module overrides like 'rate.delay' exist where the same control means something different), 'module.*' for the palette chips / canvas nodes, 'action.*' for gestures and buttons, and option-suffixed variants like 'scale.minor' or 'mode.up' for per-option tails (HelpText::keyForOption picks them when present). A missing key shows the bare message without a tail. Keep lines terse - the bar is one line and truncates. ASCII hyphens only, never an em-dash (JUCE's default font lacks the glyph).
transport.play: Starts and stops the built-in transport. Generators only run while it plays.
transport.tempo: Playback tempo for the standalone app. In a DAW the host tempo is used instead.
action.settings: Theme and audition-synth options. The patch keeps running underneath.
root: The home note of the key. Scale degrees are counted from it.
root.global: Follows the Root set in the menu bar.
scale: The set of notes the music is built from.
scale.global: Follows the Scale set in the menu bar.
scale.off: No scale - this module works in plain semitones.
scale.major: Bright and happy. The standard major scale.
scale.minor: Dark and moody. The standard (natural) minor scale.
scale.dorian: Minor with a brighter, jazzy lift. Common in folk and funk.
scale.phrygian: Dark and tense, with a Spanish edge.
scale.lydian: Major with a dreamy, floating quality.
scale.mixolydian: Major with a bluesy, dominant edge. Common in rock.
scale.locrian: Dark and unstable; rarely used as a home key.
scale.pentatonic: Five-note major scale. Open, with no clashing notes.
scale.chromatic: All twelve notes - no key at all.
module.midiin: Feeds the notes you play into the patch. Its channel setting filters what gets in.
module.output: Sends the finished stream out of the plugin. A patch with no Output is silent.
module.random: Draws random notes from a scale, inside a note range, on a steady step grid.
module.scale: Walks a scale up or down, step by step - an instant melodic run.
module.lfo: Sweeps pitch along a wave shape over bars, played as notes on a step grid.
module.chord: Plays one diatonic chord on a bar cycle - pick its degree, type and inversion.
module.drone: Holds a long sustained voicing for bars at a time - a bed under the patch.
module.arp: Turns held notes into a running arpeggio.
module.rhythmize: Retriggers held notes on a 16-step pattern. Switch steps off to carve a groove.
module.quantize: Pulls passing notes onto a timing grid, with optional swing.
module.scalemod: Snaps passing notes onto a scale, so everything stays in key.
module.progression: Moves everything through a chord progression, one step at a time.
module.shift: Transposes passing notes - in scale steps, or semitones with Scale Off.
module.mirror: Flips notes upside down around a centre and keeps them in a register window.
module.harmonizer: Stacks a chord on top of every passing note.
module.delay: Repeats each note as fading echoes on the beat grid, with an optional pitch climb.
module.strum: Fans a chord's notes out one after another, like a strummed guitar.
module.humanize: Adds performance feel - swing, lay-back, accents and small random variation.
action.add: Double-click it to open its settings; drag from its right-edge port to wire it up.
action.connect: Notes now flow along the cable. Click a cable to select it; the tray removes dragged-in modules.
action.cable: Tap the X badge or press Delete to remove the connection.
action.window: Changes are applied right away, so you can listen while you edit. Cancel undoes them all.
action.filter: Shows or hides this family of modules in the tray.
channel.in: Which incoming MIDI channel enters the patch. All takes every channel.
channel.out: The MIDI channel stamped on everything this Output sends.
rate: The step grid new notes fire on.
rate.quantize: The grid notes are pulled onto.
rate.humanize: The grid Swing and Accent lock to.
rate.rhythmize: The length of one pattern step. At 1/16 the 16 steps span exactly one bar.
gate: How much of each step the note sounds. 100 percent is fully legato; lower stutters.
octaves: How many octaves the pattern climbs through before it repeats.
repeat: Restarts the pattern from the top after this long. Endless never restarts.
repeat.strum: Re-strums a chord you keep holding, this often. Endless strums only when new notes arrive.
holdLength: How long the sound lasts. Shorter than Repeat leaves silence before the next one.
holdRepeat: How often a new one starts. Endless runs back-to-back with no rest.
from: The lowest note it may pick.
to: The highest note it may pick.
endOn: Whether the walk finishes on the octave root or on the scale's last degree.
shape: The wave the pitch follows over one cycle.
shape.saw-up: A steady climb, then a jump back down.
shape.saw-down: A steady fall, then a jump back up.
shape.square: Jumps between high and low, half a cycle each.
shape.random: A fresh random value for every note. Cycle length and phase do not apply.
cycle: How long one full sweep lasts, in bars.
depthOct: How far the sweep swings around its centre, in octaves.
depthSteps: Extra sweep depth in scale steps, added on top of the octaves.
phase: Where in the sweep the cycle begins, in degrees.
swing: Pushes every second step late for a shuffle feel. Around 67 percent is the classic triplet shuffle.
amount: How far notes move - scale steps with a scale, semitones with Scale Off.
feedback: Each echo's loudness as a share of the one before. Higher means more echoes; 0 means none.
shift: Each echo lands this far above or below the one before, so the tail climbs or falls.
center: The pivot: notes flip around this pitch, high becoming low. Off turns the flip off.
low: The bottom of the register window notes must stay in.
high: The top of the register window notes must stay in.
bounds: What happens to a note that falls outside the Low-High window.
bounds.limit: Notes outside the window are dropped.
bounds.mirror: Notes outside the window fold back inside across the nearest edge.
degree: Which scale degree the chord is built on.
type.triad: Root, third and fifth - the plain chord.
type.sus2: The third is replaced by the second - open and neutral.
type.sus4: The third is replaced by the fourth - tense, wants to resolve.
type.5th: Root and fifth only - a power chord, neither major nor minor.
type.octave: Just the octave above - the classic octaver.
type.octave+5th: The fifth and the octave above.
inversion: How many of the lowest chord tones move up an octave, changing the bottom note.
harmmode: How several held notes are treated.
harmmode.add: Every held note gets its own chord.
harmmode.replace: Only the newest note sounds - one chord at a time.
harmmode.top: Only the highest held note is harmonized; the rest pass through plain.
voicing.root+5th: Root plus the fifth - wide and stable.
spread: The gap between one fanned note and the next, synced to tempo. Off passes chords straight through.
direction: Which end of the chord leads the strum.
direction.up: Low note first, like a downstroke.
direction.down: High note first, like an upstroke.
curve: How the gaps between the fanned notes are spaced.
curve.accelerate: The fan speeds up - notes bunch toward the end.
curve.decelerate: The fan slows down - notes bunch at the start.
velTilt: Tilts loudness across the fan - negative fades it away, positive swells it up.
jitter: Loosens each note's timing and touch a little, so strums are not machine-perfect.
layback: Drags every note slightly behind the beat - a relaxed, unhurried feel.
accent: Plays the strong beats louder, so the groove has emphasis.
timeJit: Small random timing wobble, note by note. Repeats identically every loop.
velJit: Small random loudness differences, note by note.
lenJit: Small random note-length differences, note by note.
progRate: How long one progression step lasts.
prog.step: Edit the selected step with the Degree and Octave boxes below.
prog.degree: The scale degree this step moves everything to. I leaves it unchanged.
prog.octave: Shifts this step by whole octaves as well.
prog.add: The arrows on the last cell add and remove steps.
prog.remove: The arrows on the last cell add and remove steps.
rhythm.step: Active steps retrigger the notes you hold; silent steps leave a gap.
theme: Switches the editor's colors. Pure presentation - the MIDI is untouched.
synth.enabled: A built-in preview sound, so the patch is audible on its own. Current's real output is MIDI.
synth.character: Tone color of the preview synth, from clean to fat.
synth.cutoff: How bright the preview synth sounds.
synth.envamt: How much extra brightness each note starts with.
synth.decay: How long each preview note rings before fading away.
synth.space: Adds delay and reverb around the preview synth.
